name: nbtscan-unixwiz
title: nbtscan-unixwiz

commands.name: nbtscan-unixwiz

arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for nbtscan-unixwiz.
arguments.raw: -unixwiz  Option present in package usage line for nbtscan-unixwiz.

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# nbtscan-unixwiz -h
 nbtscan-unixwiz: invalid option -- 'h'
 nbtscan 1.0.35 - 2008-04-08 - http://www.unixwiz.net/tools/
 
 usage: nbtscan-unixwiz [options] target [targets...]
 
  Targets are lists of IP addresses, DNS names, or address
  ranges. Ranges can be in /nbits notation ("192.168.12.0/24")
  or with a range in the last octet ("192.168.12.64-97")
 
  -V        show Version information
  -f        show Full NBT resource record responses (recommended)
  -H        generate HTTP headers
  -v        turn on more Verbose debugging
  -n        No looking up inverse names of IP addresses responding
  -p <n>    bind to UDP Port <n> (default=0)
  -m        include MAC address in response (implied by '-f')
  -T <n>    Timeout the no-responses in <n> seconds (default=2 secs)
  -w <n>    Wait <n> msecs after each write (default=10 ms)
  -t <n>    Try each address <n> tries (default=1)
  -P        generate results in perl hashref format

examples.command:
nbtscan-unixwiz -n 192.168.0.100-110
192.168.0.105   WORKGROUP\RETROPIE              SHARING
*timeout (normal end of scan)

nbtscan-unixwiz -f 192.168.0.38
192.168.0.38    WORKGROUP\DOOKOSSEL             SHARING
  DOOKOSSEL      <00> UNIQUE Workstation Service
  DOOKOSSEL      <03> UNIQUE Messenger Service<3>
  DOOKOSSEL      <20> UNIQUE File Server Service
  ..__MSBROWSE__.<01> GROUP  Master Browser
  WORKGROUP      <00> GROUP  Domain Name
  WORKGROUP      <1d> UNIQUE Master Browser
  WORKGROUP      <1e> GROUP  Browser Service Elections
  00:00:00:00:00:00   ETHER

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=ee899cad2ee4553fd692f5331fa3b57a225a7913, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
Scanner for open NETBIOS nameservers
This package contains a command-line tool that scans for open NETBIOS
nameservers on a local or remote TCP/IP network, and this is a first step in
finding of open shares. It is based on the functionality of the standard
Windows tool nbtstat, but it operates on a range of addresses instead of just
one.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use nbtscan-unixwiz because ### nbtscan-unixwiz.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `nbtscan-unixwiz [options] target [targets...]` as documented upstream for nbtscan-unixwiz.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair nbtscan-unixwiz with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `nbtscan-unixwiz --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -unixwiz
arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for nbtscan-unixwiz.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install nbtscan-unixwiz. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `nbtscan-unixwiz [options] target [targets...]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### nbtscan-unixwiz.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install nbtscan-unixwiz
